message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The goal of the lockdown is to mitigate and if possible prevent the spread of an epidemic. It consists in reducing social interactions. This is taken into account by the introduction of a factor of reduction of social interactions q, and by decreasing the transmission coefficient of the disease accordingly. Evaluating
                  <jats:italic>q</jats:italic>
                  is a difficult question and one can ask if it makes sense to compute an average coefficient
                  <jats:italic>q</jats:italic>
                  for a given population, in order to make predictions on the basic reproduction rate ℛ
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  , the dynamics of the epidemic or the fraction of the population that will have been infected by the end of the epidemic. On a very simple example, we show that the computation of ℛ
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  in a heterogeneous population is not reduced to the computation of an average
                  <jats:italic>q</jats:italic>
                  but rather to the direct computation of an average coefficient ℛ
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  . Even more interesting is the fact that, in a range of data compatible with the Covid-19 outbreak, the size of the epidemic is deeply modified by social heterogeneity, as is the height of the epidemic peak, while the date at which it is reached mainly depends on the average ℛ
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  coefficient. This paper illustrates more technical results that can be found in [4], with new numerical computations. It is intended to draw the attention on the role of heterogeneities in a population in a very simple case, which might be difficult to apprehend in more realistic but also more complex models.
                </jats:p>
title: Social heterogeneity and the COVID-19 lockdown in a multi-group SEIR model

reference.article-title: Un modèle mathématique des débuts de l’épidémie de coronavirus en France
The construction of next-generation matrices for compartmental epidemic models
reference.journal-title: Journal of The Royal Society Inter-face

reference.article-title: Heterogeneous social interactions and the COVID-19 lock-down outcome in a multi-group SEIR model

reference.author: EpiCov
reference.journal-title: En mai 2020, 4,5 % de la population en France métropolitaine a développé des anticorps contre le SARS-CoV-2, Etudes & Résultats

reference.article-title: Evolutionary game theory modelling to represent the behavioural dynamics of economic shutdowns and shield immunity in the COVID-19 pandemic

reference.unstructured: Santé Publique France, French national public health agency. https://dashboard.covid19.data.gouv.fr, 2020.
